title: The Battle of Cadinth

"'''The Battle of Cadinth'''" is a short story written by [[Bill Smith]]. It was first published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 2|''Star Wars Galaxy'' #2]] by [[Topps]] in Winter 1995. It takes place [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], and also features [[roleplaying game]] stats for various [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and vehicles to use the story in a role-playing game. On [[February 23]], 2007, the story was published on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Hyperspace'']].

The story features droids and vehicles based on concepts created by [[Kenner Products|Kenner]] for a proposed toy line set after [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] called ''[[The Epic Continues]]'', which was ultimately rejected by [[Lucasfilm]]. Many of these toy concepts were featured in an article published in the same issue of ''Star Wars Galaxy''.

==Plot summary==
An [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] battalion begins attacking a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] outpost on the spaceport world of [[Cadinth/Legends|Cadinth]]. As Republic pilots dash to their [[Defender starfighter|Defenders]], Imperial [[Sniper airspeeder]]s begin flying down onto the battlefield. Several hundred [[AP-2 attack droid|AP-2]], [[AP-3 attack droid|AP-3]] and [[AP-1-C attack droid]]s gun down the lightly defended Republic infantry, as the Imperial Snipers gain the same advantage in the air. Republic [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Brin Stiels]] contacts Republic Core Base for reinforcements, soon choking on smoke and collapsing. Just as a group of [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]] appear on the horizon and target his position, the story ends.
